Hardware-Accelerated VCQs in RISC-V IOMMU Proposal of Work

Jiankang Chen
Mar 18   #262  

Hardware-Accelerated VCQs in RISC-V IOMMU Proposal of Work
Proposer

Name: CHEN Jiankang & GUO Ren & XUE Shuai

Organization/Affiliation: Alibaba 

Introduction

The current RISC-V IOMMU specification defines a single Command Queue (CQ) interface, which becomes a critical bottleneck in high-performance computing environments. This proposal presents solutions to address these limitations while maintaining backward compatibility with existing implementations.

The mission of this task group is to define hardware-accelerated virtual command queues (VCQs) in the RISC-V IOMMU to reduce the virtualization overhead caused by the single-CQ design.

Why Existing Extensions Are Insufficient?

Scalability Challenges in Multi-Core Systems

In modern server SoCs with high core counts (e.g., EPYC Turin, with up to 192 cores), a single command queue protected by a spinlock creates a severe serialization bottleneck. When multiple CPUs simultaneously issue IOTLB invalidation commands, lock contention leads to:

  • Performance degradation proportional to core count
  • Increased latency for memory management operations
  • Poor scalability for I/O-intensive workloads

Virtualization Overhead

Current virtualization support relies on a trap-and-emulate model in which:

  • Every guest command submission triggers a VM exit
  • The hypervisor must intercept, validate, and proxy commands
  • Each operation incurs significant overhead

AI and HPC Workload Requirements

Contemporary data center, AI, and high-performance computing workloads place unprecedented demands on IOMMU architectures. These requirements extend beyond traditional device isolation to enable new computational paradigms. Representative use cases include:

  • Guest IO-protection
  • Shared Virtual Memory (SVM) applications inside VMs: modern AI frameworks such as NVIDIA CUDA and AMD ROCm require unified address spaces between CPUs and GPUs
  • DPDK support inside VMs

Definitions

  • CQ тАФ IOMMU Command Queue
  • SVM тАФ Shared Virtual Memory
  • ECMDQ тАФ Enhanced Command Queue in Arm SMMUv3
  • VF тАФ Virtual Function used by a virtual machine
  • LCQ тАФ Logical Command Queue in a VF

Background

Solutions to Single Command Queue Contention

SMMUv3.3-ECMDQ Optional [3] support for Enhanced Command queue interfaces to solve the lock contention. HiSilicon Kunpeng and Marvell SoCs implement the SMMU ECMDQ feature and have shown an approximately 5% improvement compared with SMMUs using software batching support [1].[1]

NVIDIA's Tegra241 (Grace) SoC has a CMDQ-Virtualization (CMDQV) hardware that extends standard ARM SMMUv3 to support multiple command queues with virtualization capabilities. CMDQV also allows the kernel to use multiple VCMDQs, delivering limited but measurable performance improvements: up to a 20% reduction in TLB invalidation time was observed in a multi-threaded DMA unmap benchmark, compared with a single queue [2].

Solutions to Virtualization Overhead

Hardware-assisted virtual IOMMUs have emerged as the industry solution for eliminating trap-and-emulate overhead by providing each guest with dedicated command queue resources.

NVIDIA's Tegra241 (Grace) Virtual Command Queue [2] enables direct guest command submission with a focused set of supported commands, allowing the IOMMU hardware to directly access guest-owned queue memory. This approach delivers substantial performance gains: DMA unmap tests demonstrate 70% to 90% reduction in TLB invalidation time compared to traditional nested SMMU implementations that require hypervisor trapping.

AMD vIOMMU, available since the 2nd generation AMD EPYC (Rome) platform, provides comprehensive hardware acceleration through three dedicated buffersтАФCommand Buffer, Event Log Buffer, and PPR Log Buffer [4]. Each buffer allows guest VMs to directly control hardware queues without hypervisor intervention. Production measurements show significant performance gains: storage workloads achieve 1.67x higher bandwidth (FIO random read), while network throughput increases by 3.49x (NETPERF TCP_STREAM) compared to virtio-IOMMU implementations [5].

These implementations share a fundamental principle: IOMMU hardware directly accesses guest-controlled queue memory, eliminating VM exits for common operations. The consistent performance improvements observed across vendorsтАФfrom 1.67├Ч to 3.5├Ч for real workloadsтАФdemonstrate that hardware acceleration is essential for competitive I/O performance in virtualized environments.

Objectives

This task group will propose a hardware-assisted multi-CQ capability for the IOMMU specification.

The hardware provides multiple physical CQs, enabling parallel command execution and priority handling. Each physical CQ has two 64 KB MMIO pages.

The physical Command Queue (CQ) pool is flexibly allocated to vIOMMU interfaces (VFs). Each VF serves as an allocation unit dedicated to a single virtual machine. Physical CQs are allocated to vIOMMU interfaces and exposed within each VF as Logical Command Queues (LCQs). VFs are equipped with configuration mechanisms for managing parameters such as GSCID and RID.

Within a VF, we introduce logical CQ support to map physical CQs, where each logical CQ maps to a unique physical CQ. The Page 0 and Page 1 register layout of a logical CQ is identical to that of a physical CQ.

To improve virtual machine performance, a single VF may support multiple logical CQs, which effectively allows that VF to use multiple physical CQs.

To ensure hardware isolation, each VF is designed to be uniquely associated with a GSCID or PSCID.

Exclusions

The TG will not consider vPASID and IOMMU GIPC (G-stage table in the Process Table) features. The TG will also not consider IOMMU dirty logging, or live migration. These can be considered as part of future additional TGs or existing SIGs.

Collaborations

  • Datacenter SIG
  • IOMMU SIG

Governing Committee

Privileged ISA Committee (IC)

Sponsoring Organizations

The following Premier and Strategic Members support this proposal:

  1. Alibaba (Premier) - CHEN Jiankang & GUO Ren & XUE Shuai

  • previous page
  • #262
  • next page
